topic: Paper 1: Explorations in Creative Reading and Writing
subject: english-language

items.q: What is overextension in child language?
items.correct: When a child applies a word more broadly than adults do — e.g. calling all four-legged animals 'dog' before learning distinct categories.
items.misconception: Overextension is when children use very long words they don't understand.
items.random_wrong: Overextension is when children speak too loudly.
items.why: Overextension is about semantic categories, not word length or volume. It shows children are forming hypotheses about meaning, then refining them — a key window into acquisition.

items.correct: A dialect that has gained social and institutional prestige — used in education and formal writing; it is one variety, not inherently superior in linguistic terms.
items.misconception: Standard English is the original and correct form of the language.
items.random_wrong: Standard English is the same as Received Pronunciation.
items.why: Standard English is one historical dialect (originally East Midlands) that gained prestige. It is not older or more logical than other varieties. Students confuse standard with RP (Standard is grammar/lexis; RP is accent).

items.q: What is the difference between a dialect and an accent?
items.correct: Accent refers only to pronunciation; dialect includes vocabulary and grammar as well as pronunciation — so all dialects involve accents but not vice versa.
items.misconception: Dialect and accent mean the same thing.
items.random_wrong: A dialect is a foreign language; an accent is how locals speak.
items.why: The two terms are commonly conflated in everyday use. In linguistics, accent is a subset of dialect — dialect is broader, covering all variation, accent only sound.

items.q: What does the L in PEEL stand for?
items.why: It's Link. “List” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which reading skill hunts specific facts?
items.why: It's Scanning. “Skimming” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How many letters in PEEL paragraph structure?
items.why: Exact answer: 4 letters. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
